claim.content: Comparing two trial-wave-function protocols for quasiparticle-band calculations of paramagnetic 2D-UEL—(A) using a single trial wave function optimized for the ground state and applied to all k vectors, and (B) reoptimizing the trial wave function by energy minimization separately for each excited-state k-point—shows that reoptimization (B) slightly increases the VMC estimate of the quasiparticle effective mass m* relative to protocol (A), whereas the corresponding impact on fixed-node DMC m* is negligible within statistical uncertainty for the tested cases (for example N = 146 and r_s = 1, 2, 5).
